description:
A Vision Scientist has collected Multiple Object Tracking (MOT) data from 15 participants for an attentional capacity study. The data is located at ~/pebl/data/mot_tracking_data.csv and includes subject_id, trial, target_count, distractor_count, speed_deg_sec, targets_clicked, and rt_sec.

Compute the chance-adjusted tracking capacity (K) for each trial, defined as the target count multiplied by the adjusted accuracy. Adjusted accuracy is calculated as (observed accuracy - chance) / (1 - chance), where observed accuracy is targets_clicked/target_count and chance is target_count/(target_count + distractor_count). Any negative adjusted accuracy values must be set to zero.

Aggregate the results to find the mean K capacity for each participant per target_count condition (2, 3, 4, 5, 6) and their overall mean reaction time in milliseconds. Exclude any participant whose mean K capacity at target_count 6 is strictly greater than 5.5.

Export the results to ~/pebl/analysis/mot_capacity_report.json. The file must include a 'participants' list (containing ID, mean K values, and mean reaction time, or exclusion details for the cheater) and a 'group_mean_capacity' dictionary for target counts 2 through 6, excluding the cheater's data. Finally, save a line plot visualizing the Group Mean Capacity vs. Target Count to ~/pebl/analysis/capacity_plot.png.
